jokes.text: A delivery of chili cheese fries? They'll be mush by the time they get here. No way.
jokes.setup: Character ordering food for delivery at a bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Dennis's practical but unnecessary correction about food logistics
jokes.explanation: Dennis being unnecessarily pedantic about food delivery is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: A delivery of chili cheese fries? They'll be mush by the time they get here. No way.

jokes.text: Well, finesse isn't really my style, Charlie. I like to dominate the table into submission.
jokes.setup: Charlie advising Mac to use more finesse in pool
jokes.punchline: Mac's over-the-top masculine response about 'dominating' a pool table
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac dialogue - turning pool into some masculine power fantasy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, finesse isn't really my style, Charlie. I like to dominate the table into submission.

jokes.text: looking like Carmen Sandiego
jokes.setup: Dee wearing a mysterious coat and acting secretive
jokes.punchline: Dennis comparing her appearance to the iconic detective character
jokes.explanation: Solid visual reference joke, economical but not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: looking like Carmen Sandiego

jokes.text: Visual gag: Dee reveals bowling shirt under coat after dramatic buildup
jokes.setup: Extended sequence of everyone demanding to see what Dee's hiding under her coat
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic reveal of just a bowling shirt
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed misdirection with good buildup and satisfying deflation

dialogue.line: Visual gag: Dee reveals bowling shirt under coat after dramatic buildup

jokes.text: As if. As if, Dee. As if. As if. As if, Dee. As if.
jokes.setup: Dee asking why they'd want to get involved in bowling
jokes.punchline: All three guys repeating 'As if' in overlapping, dismissive chorus
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective group dynamic joke, shows their collective dismissiveness

dialogue.line: As if. As if, Dee. As if. As if. As if, Dee. As if.

jokes.text: My ding-dong wasn't even in my hand. I mean, what do you... Mine was. Yeah, so was mine. Yeah, I was scratching mine when she said that. I-I had my hands fully down my pants, if I'm being honest. I had to push it to the left.
jokes.setup: Dee's insult about them having their 'ding-dongs' in their hands
jokes.punchline: Escalating admissions that they were literally all touching themselves
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating reveal that makes Dee's random insult accidentally prophetic. Each admission gets more specific and uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: My ding-dong wasn't even in my hand. I mean, what do you...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Mine was.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, so was mine.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was scratching mine when she said that.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I-I had my hands fully down my pants, if I'm being honest. I had to push it to the left.

jokes.text: Oh, hello, ladies. You ready to roll?
jokes.setup: Dee arriving late to her bowling team
jokes.punchline: Her overly confident greeting despite being late and unprepared
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee obliviousness to social situations

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, hello, ladies. You ready to roll?

jokes.text: It's just that our fourth didn't show up and literally no one else was free on a Friday night.
jokes.setup: Dee asking if they want her on the team
jokes.punchline: Waitress's brutal honesty about Dee being their last resort
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of devastating social truth

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: It's just that our fourth didn't show up and literally no one else was free on a Friday night.

jokes.text: I sucked off a rando in the parking lot. Ugh, my mouth hurts.
jokes.setup: Snail explaining why the bowling scene is 'so hot'
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of random sexual encounter followed by physical complaint
jokes.explanation: Peak Snail behavior - perfectly uncomfortable oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Snail
dialogue.line: I sucked off a rando in the parking lot. Ugh, my mouth hurts.

jokes.text: Frige-it Bitches
jokes.setup: Waitress shows team shirts from appliance company sponsor
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun combining 'frigid' and refrigerator company
jokes.explanation: Clever double-pun that's appropriately terrible for the characters

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Frige-it Bitches

jokes.text: Or perhaps I should let my balls do the talking.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to assert dominance over her bowling team
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about bowling balls that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Predictable double entendre but fits Dee's oblivious confidence

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Or perhaps I should let my balls do the talking.

jokes.text: Vaginas and penises together again.
jokes.setup: Learning the bowling league is coed
jokes.punchline: Dennis's clinical, oddly nostalgic way of describing mixed-gender competition
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis - reducing human interaction to crude anatomical terms with bizarre wistfulness

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Vaginas and penises together again.

jokes.text: He did one, so I thought we were doing... Okay.
jokes.setup: Dennis doing some kind of gesture
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confused attempt to follow social cues that apparently don't exist
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie misreading social situations

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He did one, so I thought we were doing... Okay.

jokes.text: Ryan's got the forearms of an elephant wrangler. He could have been pro. I could have been. If not for my tragic accident.
jokes.setup: Liam explaining why they got into bowling
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison and dramatic setup for Ryan's 'tragic accident'
jokes.explanation: 'Elephant wrangler forearms' is wonderfully specific and absurd

dialogue.speaker: Liam
dialogue.line: Ryan's got the forearms of an elephant wrangler. He could have been pro. I could have been. If not for my tragic accident.

jokes.text: The story is too horrible for words, so don't ask me...
jokes.setup: Mentioning his tragic bowling accident
jokes.punchline: Clearly wanting to tell the story while claiming it's too horrible
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the later anticlimactic reveal

dialogue.line: The story is too horrible for words, so don't ask me...

jokes.text: combining them is strictly prohibited... The story is too terrible to tell.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about chili cheese fries
jokes.punchline: Another 'too terrible to tell' story about food combination
jokes.explanation: Establishes pattern of dramatic storytelling about mundane events

dialogue.line: combining them is strictly prohibited... The story is too terrible to tell.

jokes.text: Hanging Monster Dong Lucky Strike Chili Cheese Fryers
jokes.setup: Announcing the team names
jokes.punchline: The guys' ridiculously long, juvenile team name
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the guys' juvenile humor and inability to edit themselves

dialogue.line: Hanging Monster Dong Lucky Strike Chili Cheese Fryers

jokes.text: It's too many ideas, man. I told you it was stupid. I-I knew they were gonna make fun of us and they did make fun of us.
jokes.setup: Dee mocking their team name
jokes.punchline: Charlie's post-hoc criticism showing he knew it was bad all along
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie moment - voice of reason after the fact

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's too many ideas, man. I told you it was stupid. I-I knew they were gonna make fun of us and they did make fun of us.

jokes.text: Now, I'm no fan of women's sports. Who is? Yeah, they're not very popular. Nobody is. Nobody watches... Especially women. Women don't even like women's sports.
jokes.setup: Dennis about to tell story about Dee's bowling
jokes.punchline: Escalating misogynistic observations about women's sports viewership
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but effective escalation that reveals character awfulness

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now, I'm no fan of women's sports. Who is? Yeah, they're not very popular. Nobody is. Nobody watches... Especially women. Women don't even like women's sports.

jokes.text: Stride, stride, stride, execute.
jokes.setup: Mac preparing for his bowling approach
jokes.punchline: His overly technical, military-style approach to bowling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac - turning bowling into some kind of tactical operation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Stride, stride, stride, execute.

jokes.text: Ooh, my cooter's on fire for Mac.
jokes.setup: Mac bowling
jokes.punchline: Snail's crude sexual attraction announcement
jokes.explanation: Classic Snail grossness - perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Snail
dialogue.line: Ooh, my cooter's on fire for Mac.

jokes.text: Your cooter is most likely on fire from a yeast infection. Go in the bathroom, rinse it out and be better next time.
jokes.setup: Snail's sexual comment
jokes.punchline: Dee's clinical, dismissive medical diagnosis and instructions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan response that's somehow more uncomfortable than Snail's original comment

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Your cooter is most likely on fire from a yeast infection. Go in the bathroom, rinse it out and be better next time.

jokes.text: My character can. Me, I can't bowl for shit.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Artemis can bowl after she gets a strike
jokes.punchline: Her explanation that only her character Donna Brunswick can bowl
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis - so deep in method acting that she attributes real skills to her character

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: My character can. Me, I can't bowl for shit.

jokes.text: Frank celebrates getting a strike by slowly crawling under pins
jokes.setup: Frank bowls what appears to be a slow, weak ball
jokes.punchline: Ball somehow gets strike, Frank celebrates by crawling through pins
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag of Frank's weird celebration combined with impossible bowling physics

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank celebrates getting a strike by slowly crawling under pins

jokes.text: A quickie. Quickie it is. My character is also a giant whore, which is what attracted me to the role in the first place, so...
jokes.setup: Frank and Artemis want to have quick sex
jokes.punchline: Artemis explains her character motivation for accepting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis - using method acting to justify having public sex, with complete earnestness

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: A quickie. Quickie it is. My character is also a giant whore, which is what attracted me to the role in the first place, so...

jokes.text: Hey, Tyrone, do not take my spot! Do not take my spot at the air hockey table.
jokes.setup: Charlie distracted by arcade during bowling
jokes.punchline: His increasingly frantic concern about losing his arcade game spot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - completely missing priorities and getting invested in random arcade kid drama

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, Tyrone, do not take my spot! Do not take my spot at the air hockey table.

jokes.text: By the way, your hair looks great. Charlie, shut up, I'm trying to focus. Did you, like, change your hair or something?
jokes.setup: Waitress trying to focus on bowling
jokes.punchline: Charlie tries to flirt at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie obliviousness - trying to compliment her when she clearly wants him to shut up

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: By the way, your hair looks great.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Charlie, shut up, I'm trying to focus.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Did you, like, change your hair or something?

jokes.text: Gutter ball.
jokes.setup: Dennis promising not to psychologically mess with Dee
jokes.punchline: Him immediately breaking his promise
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Dennis's earlier story - he literally can't help himself

dialogue.line: Gutter ball.

jokes.text: Gutter ball.
jokes.setup: Dee gets strike despite Dennis's interference
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately does it again on her second roll
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Dennis can't help himself, proving his complete cruelty and control

dialogue.line: Gutter ball.

jokes.text: Besides your physical limitations, if given the opportunity to either work together as a team or tear each other apart, you will always choose the latter. Because women are devious and destructive and will always default to using their insidious venom on the most sought-after victim of all... each other.
jokes.setup: Women's team arguing with each other
jokes.punchline: Dennis's elaborate pseudo-psychological analysis of women
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis - turning casual observation into elaborate misogynistic theory delivered with academic confidence

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Besides your physical limitations, if given the opportunity to either work together as a team or tear each other apart, you will always choose the latter. Because women are devious and destructive and will always default to using their insidious venom on the most sought-after victim of all... each other.

jokes.text: Because... they're stupid.
jokes.setup: Dee building up to explanation of strategy against the men
jokes.punchline: Her anticlimactic conclusion after dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - builds up to complex strategy, delivers simple insult

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Because... they're stupid.

jokes.text: Don't-don't do it. Don't do it. Just play your game. Can I just spin one? I don't care... Let me spin one. You don't need to spin it. You just play your game and we're gonna win. Okay? Do not spin it. Do not do it. Okay, fine. I won't, I won't spin it. I won't.
jokes.setup: Waitress plants seed that Mac can't spin the ball
jokes.punchline: Mac becomes obsessed with proving he can spin despite Dennis's advice
jokes.explanation: Great setup showing Mac's insecurity and inability to let go of perceived slights

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't-don't do it. Don't do it. Just play your game.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Can I just spin one? I don't care... Let me spin one.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You don't need to spin it. You just play your game and we're gonna win. Okay? Do not spin it. Do not do it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I won't, I won't spin it. I won't.

jokes.text: Stride, stride, stride, spin!
jokes.setup: Mac trying to spin the ball despite Dennis's warnings
jokes.punchline: His modified technique call that results in failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Mac's stubborn inability to listen to advice

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Stride, stride, stride, spin!

jokes.text: Hey, Tyrone, double or nothing! 'Cause there's no way you're beating me two out of three, dude!
jokes.setup: Charlie continuing to be distracted by arcade
jokes.punchline: His increasing investment in arcade competition while team needs him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie escalation - getting more invested in wrong thing at wrong time

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, Tyrone, double or nothing! 'Cause there's no way you're beating me two out of three, dude!

jokes.text: In the third act of my play, Donna rolls a gutter ball in the big match against her rival high school.
jokes.setup: Artemis rolling a gutter ball
jokes.punchline: Her explanation that it was intentional for her character development
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Artemis moment - turning bowling failure into theatrical choice

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: In the third act of my play, Donna rolls a gutter ball in the big match against her rival high school.

jokes.text: She's a terrible person. She's a bitch and she's ugly and I didn't want her on the team. And she's gross. Sure, and she's gross. But you know what? You're even worse.
jokes.setup: Waitress starting to defend Dee
jokes.punchline: Her listing all of Dee's flaws before pivoting to attack Dennis
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - seems like brutal attack on Dee, reveals it's setup for even worse attack on Dennis

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: She's a terrible person. She's a bitch and she's ugly and I didn't want her on the team. And she's gross. Sure, and she's gross. But you know what? You're even worse.

jokes.text: I summon the very fact that I am a man!
jokes.setup: Dennis prepares to punch strength machine
jokes.punchline: Invokes his gender as if it were a magical power
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis delusion - treating his gender like a superpower, perfectly absurd and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I summon the very fact that I am a man!

jokes.text: I've broken every single bone in my hand. I cannot bowl. There's nothing solid left in there. It's just bloody soup and bone broth.
jokes.setup: Dennis clearly hurt his hand on punching machine
jokes.punchline: His dramatic, gruesome description of hand injury
jokes.explanation: 'Bloody soup and bone broth' is wonderfully excessive Dennis dramatics

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I've broken every single bone in my hand. I cannot bowl. There's nothing solid left in there. It's just bloody soup and bone broth.

jokes.text: It pinched it really bad. I had a nasty bruise for a week.
jokes.setup: Extended dramatic buildup about Ryan's 'tragic' bowling accident
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic reveal that he just pinched his finger
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to long-running setup - classic anticlimax after dramatic buildup

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: It pinched it really bad. I had a nasty bruise for a week.

jokes.text: Visual gag: Ryan gets his hand caught in ball return again
jokes.setup: Ryan bowling despite his traumatic finger-pinching history
jokes.punchline: History immediately repeating itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual callback - the exact thing he was traumatized by happens again

jokes.text: Oh, hi, Dee. I'm in here, too. Gutter ball.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to bowl her final shot
jokes.punchline: Dennis appears in Dee's head to psychologically sabotage her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Dennis's control is so complete he's literally in her head now. Perfect psychological horror comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, hi, Dee. I'm in here, too. Gutter ball.

jokes.text: Oh, hi, Dee. I'm in here, too. Gutter ball.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to focus on her crucial shot
jokes.punchline: Frank's voice apparently coming from inside her head, joining the psychological warfare
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - Frank somehow joining Dennis's psychological attack from inside Dee's head

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, hi, Dee. I'm in here, too. Gutter ball.

jokes.text: Frank's having a threesome in the bathroom with Gail and Artemis, and, um, yeah, I think the guys went to the Sixers game.
jokes.setup: Dee asking where everyone went after her triumph
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Frank's bathroom threesome alongside guys leaving for basketball
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of disturbing information treated as mundane

dialogue.line: Frank's having a threesome in the bathroom with Gail and Artemis, and, um, yeah, I think the guys went to the Sixers game.

jokes.text: Um... yeah, honestly? I don't give a shit about women's sports.
jokes.setup: Dee asking why Waitress isn't celebrating their victory
jokes.punchline: Waitress echoing the guys' earlier sentiment about women's sports
jokes.explanation: Dark perfect ending - even the women don't care about women's sports, making Dee's triumph meaningless

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Um... yeah, honestly? I don't give a shit about women's sports.
